title: thalictrum angustifolium var. simplex
intro: Thalictrum angustifolium var. simplex, also known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) that is native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and flowers have been consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Thalictrum angustifolium var. simplex can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
